free_text: Measurement of Secondary Neutron Emission Double
Differential Cross Section for Natural Iron Induced
by 8.17 MeV Neutron
in Chinese
The measured raw TOF spectra are
included gas-in(sample- in/sample-out) and
gas-out(sample-in/sample-out). The net TOF spectra
are determined by gas-in/gas-out, and including the
propagation of uncertainties. The flight paths for 3
main detectors are 607.08, 653.48, 509.08 cm,
respectively. The flight path of monitor is 600.0 cm,
and set at 60 degree.
The 13MV HI-13 Tandem Accelerator in
China Institute of Atomic Energy (CIAE).
Three main detectors BC501A 17.78 x 10.16 cm
in diameter and height with 160 keVee (electronic
energy) electron threshold, and one stilbene monitor
3.0 x 3.0 cm in diameter and height with 950 keVee
were used.
For reduction of the experimental data, a detailed
Monte Carlo simulation was employed to obtain TOF
spectra, which were compared with the experimental
TOF spectra. The corrections due to the finite
sample size effects, flux attenuation and multiple
scattering, etc., were made with Monte Carlo
simulation.
Deuterium gas target had following parameters:
40 x 10 mm in length and diameter, the thickness of
gold backing is 0.5 mm, the thickness of entrance Mo
foil is 10 um. The gas pressure is 0.6 MPa.
Fe (Natural): height=35 mm; inner diameter=10 mm;
outer diameter=30 mm; net weight=171.526 g;
Polyethylene: height=30 mm; diameter=25 mm; net
weight=13.960 g; purify > 99%;
The total uncertainty is (5-10%);
The uncertainty of neutron detection
efficiency is (3%);
The uncertainty of the correction for multiple
scattering is (3%);
The uncertainty of n-p scattering cross
section is (1%);
The uncertainty of scattering angle is
less than 0.5 degree;
The counting statistical uncertainty (<5%);
n-p monitor cross section (1%)
Data were received from author.
Entry was approved by author.
Compiled by Guochang CHEN;
Integral from double differential cross
sections
Elastic peak in the measured TOF spectra contains
the first inelastic scattering of Fe-56 (Q=-0.847
MeV). To obtain elastic scattering angular
distribution, the 1st inelastic scattering angular
distribution of S.M.El-Kadi et al. (Nucl. Phys. A,
1982,390:509-540), W.E.Kinney et al. (ORNL-
4515,1970) and A.B.Smith (Nucl. Phys. A,
1996,605:269-289) were adopted to subtract this
influence.
Double differential cross section
